items.q: How many semitones are in a perfect fifth?
items.why: Students count fifth as 'five steps' but the name refers to letter NAMES (1-2-3-4-5), not semitones. C to G spans 5 letter names but 7 semitones (C-C#-D-D#-E-F-F#-G).

items.q: How does a harmonic minor scale differ from a natural minor?
items.correct: The seventh degree is raised by a semitone (creating a leading note).
items.misconception: All notes are raised by a semitone.
items.why: Students think the whole scale changes. Only the 7TH is altered to create a leading note, which strengthens cadences in minor keys - producing the characteristic augmented second between 6th and 7th.

items.q: How many crotchets are in a semibreve?
items.why: Students confuse the relative durations. A semibreve (whole note) = 2 minims = 4 crotchets = 8 quavers. Each level halves in length.
It's Schoenberg. “Stravinsky” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which composer is most associated with minimalist works such as 'Music for 18 Musicians'?
items.why: Correct answer: Reich. “Glass” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year did John Cage compose his silent work '4'33"'?
items.why: Exact answer: 1952. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
